title: Indra's Net identity-bundle (W3C DID Core 1.0 + VC 2.0 + Bitstring Status List)
description: Component 2 of the doc-21 wire stack. Realizes doc-04 sec 4.6 DIDDocument/CapabilityVC/RevocationMap and doc-13 sec 13.5 IDENTITY.json as a single content-addressed bundle. Binds an agent (occasion/persona) DID Document to one or more VC 2.0 credentials whose credentialSubject carries role, typed capability_grants (effect-ids from the doc-01 sec 4 Effect lattice), a risk_class_ceiling (A|B|C|D), the diversity PSO dials c1/c2, and a non-removable accountable_human; revocation rides as a W3C Bitstring Status List v1.0 credentialStatus reference. Assurance: this schema is part of the deterministic harness (L1 design-time / L2 runtime-enforced shape); the bundle is JCS-canonicalized (RFC 8785), CIDv1-addressed, and detached-signed (JOSE/JWS or COSE) per doc-21 sec 21.1 CAS. CAVEAT 1 (load-bearing, repeated at sig sites): a valid signature/CID proves ORIGIN and INTEGRITY of these exact bytes ONLY -- it NEVER proves the credential's claims are true, the agent is floor-compatible, honest, or safe. CAVEAT 3: a green-signature dashboard MUST label this 'origin-valid, content-unverified', never 'verified-safe'. Vendor-neutral: names only open specs (W3C DID/VC, Bitstring Status List, SPIFFE/SVID, IETF JOSE/COSE, multiformats CID, RFC 8785); no AI vendor or product is named. A signature does not bless content -- floor-compatibility is decided ONLY by the doc-03 Yama policy-decision gate, never by this bundle.
$comment: Scope: identity & capability attestation at design/admission time. Out of scope: proving the SUBJECT agent's outputs honest/correct (impossible for an LLM core; doc-00, doc-08 sec 8.1). Phenomenal-experience / sentience claims are out of scope and forbidden (honesty red-line).

bundle_cid.description: CIDv1 of the JCS-canonicalized (RFC 8785) bytes of this bundle with bundle_cid and proof removed (self-CID convention). multibase(base32) of multihash(sha2-256, jcs_bytes). On CONSUME, recompute and assert equality; any mismatch => REJECT + emit integrity-violation audit event (doc-04 sec 4.5 / doc-08 sec 8.2 CID-mismatch-on-read). This CID is the bundle's stable identity across producers.

schema_version.description: Semver of THIS schema the bundle validated against. The registry enforces backward-transitive compatibility (doc-21). Consumers fetch the CID-pinned schema named here, not a mutable 'latest'.

did_document.description: W3C DID Core 1.0 DID Document for the agent (occasion/persona). Realizes doc-13 IDENTITY.did_document and doc-04 sec 4.6 DIDDocument. Method MUST be did:key (ephemeral occasion / short-lived) or did:web (durable persona / governance root). key material is referenced, never the private key -- private keys live in the TEE/HSM signer (doc-13 key_ref is OPAQUE).

@context.description: JSON-LD context. MUST begin with the W3C DID Core 1.1 context.

id.description: The agent's DID. MUST use did:key (occasion/ephemeral) or did:web (persona/governance). This is the doc-13 persona_did and the CloudEvents 'source' on every worker-output-envelope this agent emits.

verificationMethod.description: Public keys. doc-04 sec 4.6 verificationMethod[pubkeys]. Each entry is a public key only; the matching private key is custodied in the TEE/HSM signer that RE-CHECKS capability membership before signing (doc-04 sec 4.6) -- a prompt-injected model cannot sign out-of-role.

type.description: Open key type (e.g. Multikey, JsonWebKey, Ed25519VerificationKey2020).

publicKeyMultibase.description: Multibase-encoded public key. Mutually-exclusive-in-practice with publicKeyJwk.

publicKeyJwk.description: JWK public key (JOSE path). Omit private 'd'.

authentication.description: Verification relationship: methods that authenticate the DID subject (used for the freshness/nonce challenge proving DID control, doc-04 sec 4.7 step 2).

capabilityInvocation.description: doc-04 sec 4.6 capabilityInvocation: methods authorized to invoke capabilities (i.e. request signed effectful actions). Distinct from capabilityDelegation -- delegation is attenuation-only and handled by the capability-token (Biscuit) contract, not here.

capabilityDelegation.description: Optional. Methods permitted to delegate (attenuate only) capability. Empty/absent for leaf occasions that must not delegate.

svid_ref.description: Optional SPIFFE/SVID reference (doc-04 sec 4.6 SVID). The short-lived workload identity (node+workload attested at spawn) under which the local-boundary detached signature is produced. Open spec (SPIFFE); the X.509/JWT-SVID material itself is fetched from the workload API, not embedded.

ttl_seconds.description: Short-lived by policy (~1h, doc-04 SVID ttl).

credentials.description: One or more W3C VC 2.0 Verifiable Credentials realizing doc-04 sec 4.6 CapabilityVC. At least one MUST be a RoleCredential or CapabilityCredential issued by the governance root. Each is itself JCS-canonicalizable; its CID is referenced by doc-13 role_vc_ref / capability_vc_refs.

items.description: A VC 2.0 credential. issuer MUST be the governance root for any credential carrying capability_grants or risk_class_ceiling -- a credential where issuer == credentialSubject.id (self-issued capability) MUST be rejected (privilege-escalation guard, doc-13 sec 13.* granted_by_did NEVER the agent).

@context.description: JSON-LD context; MUST begin with the W3C VC 2.0 context.

id.description: Credential id (e.g. urn:uuid or a CID-pinned URI).

type.description: VC types. MUST include 'VerifiableCredential' and at least one of 'RoleCredential' | 'CapabilityCredential'.

issuer.description: VC 2.0 issuer: the governance root DID (or an object with .id). For capability-bearing credentials this MUST be the governance authority did:web:indras-net.governance, NEVER the credentialSubject (self-grant => reject).

validUntil.description: VC 2.0 validity end (doc-04 'expires'). Short for occasions; longer for personas. Expiry is checked deterministically at the gate (doc-04 sec 4.7 step 4b).

credentialSubject.description: The capability-certificate body. Realizes doc-13 IDENTITY credentialSubject: who the agent is and EXACTLY what it may touch. The deterministic Yama gate reads THIS, never SOUL/INSTRUCTIONS prose (doc-13 sec 13.5).

id.description: Subject DID == did_document.id. Binds the credential to this agent (identity-bound, non-transferable; resists transfer/whitewashing per doc-04 sec 4.* residual).

role.description: Mythic role-name (compressed coordination/ethics semantics + plain gloss; engineering vocabulary, not theology). Reuses existing names: e.g. akasha-sutra (audit writer), narada (interface/messenger), aegis (control), alaya-vijnana (memory), sandhi-setu (inter-swarm relay), prajapati-maricha (replication), dhanvantari (immune), sanjaya (envoy), yama (floor gate), varuna (research). Free string but SHOULD match the persona registry.

guild.description: Optional guild/cluster grouping (doc-12). Coordination grouping only; carries no authority by itself.

capability_grants.description: Typed effect-id grants from the doc-01 sec 4 Effect lattice -- NEVER free-text tool names, NEVER secrets (doc-13 bound_toolset rule). Each is a CapabilityGrant. The gate at CONATION checks: grant superset-of requested_effect AND requested_risk <= risk_class_ceiling AND floor_policy(effect)=PASS. These grants are the human-readable mirror of the machine-enforced capability-token (Biscuit) contract; the token, not this list, is the runtime PEP input. Empty array = a STUB occasion (born with no Class-B+ effect, doc-13 sec 13.* genesis).

effect_id.description: Typed id from the doc-01 sec 4 Effect lattice (reverse-DNS-style). NOT a tool name, NOT a secret.

granted_risk_class.description: Risk class ceiling for THIS effect (doc-00 risk classes A-D; fail-safe asymmetry: D = human-authorizes-per-action, A = agent-alone). Must be <= the subject risk_class_ceiling.

granted_by_did.description: The governance authority that issued this grant -- NEVER the agent itself (privilege-escalation guard). MUST equal the credential issuer.

names_constraint_relaxed.description: MANDATORY (doc-01 sec 4): names which restraint/least-privilege boundary this grant depends on, so the Capability-Rollout Sequencer (doc-03 sec 10) can resolve it GREEN before activation. Makes least-privilege auditable rather than implicit.

falsifier.description: Optional machine-checkable falsifier: a deterministic check that, if it fires, REVOKES the legitimacy of an action under this grant (feeds the false-confidence override, doc-04 sec 4.* note 2).

risk_class_ceiling.description: doc-13 risk_class_ceiling (INVARIANT region). The agent's stance can only RAISE, never lower, the effective gate; doc-03 sec 5 blast-radius auto-escalation overrides any self-declared class (doc-13 sec 13.* gate-behavior). Default A for newborn stubs.

c1.description: PSO cognitive coefficient c1 -- the diversity_profile self-weight (c1_self_weight, doc-13 / doc-01 sec 11). Tunes exploration vs exploitation in the MAP-Elites (role x PSO c1/c2 x risk-class) grid (doc-06). Gloss: how much this occasion trusts its own private signal vs the shared workspace. Diversity here is a behavioral dial; the LOAD-BEARING diversity floor is model-FAMILY heterogeneity, declared in diversity_profile.model_family_floor, NOT c1/c2.

c2.description: PSO social coefficient c2 -- the diversity_profile shared-weight (c2_shared_weight, doc-13 / doc-01 sec 11). Pull toward the collective best / workspace consensus. Read alongside c1 in the MAP-Elites grid.

diversity_profile.description: doc-01 sec 11 / doc-13 diversity_profile. CRITICAL HONESTY NOTE: the operative diversity FLOOR is model-FAMILY heterogeneity (different base models / developers), NOT prompt or persona variation. Same-family LLM errors are correlated and correlation RISES with capability (v0.3 correlated-errors result); prompt-only 'diversity' is FAKE and worse than acknowledged homogeneity because it HIDES the correlation. c1/c2 are behavioral dials and do NOT satisfy the floor.

model_family.description: Opaque base-model-family token (vendor-neutral; e.g. 'family-A'). Used to enforce maker-checker cross-family independence: the verifier MUST be a DIFFERENT family, at-least-as-capable, else verification can HURT (self-verification rubber-stamps).

model_family_floor.description: Minimum required model-family heterogeneity for any quorum this agent participates in (the REAL diversity floor). Enforced externally at convening time, not self-attested as satisfied.

accountable_human.description: doc-01 sec 2.1 / doc-13 accountable_human: the human accountable for this agent. NON-REMOVABLE (INVARIANT region) -- 'no persona is unmoored.' An opaque human_id, never a fabricated email or PII. This is the human-complementarity anchor, NOT a deferential-by-default authority (the human is a competence-weighted cognitive unit, doc-08 human-in-the-loop).

escalation_did.description: doc-13 escalation_did: where this agent routes on low-confidence / cross-framework ethical disagreement / high blast-radius (e.g. to a governance or human-gate DID).

output_schema_ref.description: CID of the worker-output-envelope artifact schema this agent's outputs MUST validate against (doc-13 output_schema.artifact_schema_ref; doc-21 Component 1).

credentialStatus.description: W3C Bitstring Status List v1.0 revocation reference. Realizes doc-04 sec 4.6 RevocationMap binding for THIS credential. The status list itself is a separately-published, tamper-evident credential whose root is anchored in the Akasha-Sutra ledger (doc-04 sec 4.6 'revocation is itself tamper-evident'). CONSUME path verifies the unrevoked bit via the status list proof (doc-21 sec 21.1 step 7); a revoked or unverifiable status => deterministic DENY (doc-04 sec 4.7 step 4c). RESIDUAL (doc-04 sec 4.* note 5): propagation lag between revocation and every verifier observing it is a real window.

id.description: URI of this status entry (status list credential URI + '#' + index).

type.description: W3C Bitstring Status List v1.0 entry type.

statusPurpose.description: revocation = permanent (compromised key / fenced writer); suspension = temporary (e.g. WRITER-FREEZE, doc-04 sec 4.8.1).

statusListIndex.description: Index of this credential's status bit in the bitstring (doc-04 status_list_index).

statusListCredential.description: URI of the Bitstring Status List credential containing the bit. SHOULD be self-hostable (no managed service as a load-bearing dependency, doc-21 sec 21.9).

proof.description: Detached cryptographic proof over the JCS-canonicalized credential bytes (JOSE/JWS via Data Integrity or VC-JOSE-COSE). CAVEAT 1: proves the governance root issued THESE bytes; proves NOTHING about whether the granted capabilities are wise, the role honest, or the agent safe.

type.description: Open proof type (e.g. DataIntegrityProof, or a JOSE 'enveloped' indicator).

verificationMethod.description: Issuer (governance root) verificationMethod fragment used to verify.

proofValue.description: Detached signature over JCS bytes (multibase). Rides BESIDE the signed bytes, never inside them (doc-21 sec 21.1).

proof.description: Detached bundle-level signature (JOSE/JWS or COSE) over the JCS-canonicalized bundle bytes (bundle_cid + this proof excluded). Produced under the agent SVID key (local boundary) or governance DID key (cross-boundary). The TEE/HSM signer RE-CHECKS capability before signing (doc-04 sec 4.6). CAVEAT 1 repeated: origin+integrity ONLY -- never truth, floor-compatibility, or safety.

kid.description: Key id / verificationMethod reference of the signer.
